title: dianthus caryophyllus subsp. gasparrinii
intro: Dianthus caryophyllus subsp. gasparrinii, also known as the pink carnation, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the carnation family. It is used in herbal medicine due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to have medicinal effects in treating wounds, reducing fever, and relieving digestive issues. The health benefits of Dianthus caryophyllus subsp. gasparrinii include its ability to soothe respiratory problems, reduce menstrual cramps, and alleviate anxiety. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to harness its medicinal properties for various health benefits.
